text: Hearing those words inside this modern, utilitarian space, I feel a quiet resignation and resolve. The first room reflects a sense of duty and routine; its clean lines and functional details hint at tasks to be done rather than moments to be savored. Moving into the second image—a vibrant, informative wall focused on research and development—the monotony is pierced by a sense of industry and curiosity. Yet the underlying tone is still one of obligation: this is the work that must be done, even if behind the curiosity is a sense of necessity more than joy. I feel that pull between duty and discovery, aware of the sometimes-blurred line between what is required and what could be inspired.
